title: The Confession of Peter
subtitle: At Caesarea Philippi, the Lord asks: "Who do you say that I am?"
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
whom blessed Peter confessed to be the true Son of God,
You too do I, unworthy, confess with my whole heart and mouth,

title: In the Beginning Was the Word
subtitle: Before Mary, before Lucifer, before the world: the eternal birth of the Son from the Father
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, eternal Word of the Father,
you were in the beginning, the Principle from the Principle.
With thee is the Principle in the day of thy strength, in the splendours of the Saints;

title: The Remedy Devised
subtitle: Mercy and Truth contend before God for the ruined race of Adam
prayerPreview: I beseech You, O Lord, let the word You have spoken be fulfilled.
Why, O Lord, have You made me?
For Truth herself knows that I perish, if You never show mercy.

title: The Flowering Rod
subtitle: How Mary, vowed to God, was betrothed to Joseph when his rod alone burst into bloom
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, flower out of the root of Jesse,
You did not violate the Virgin, but adorned her the more with gifts and graces.
You are the flower in the promise, the fruit in grace and truth;

title: The Forerunner Promised
subtitle: Gabriel at the altar of incense, and the word "Fear not, Zacharias"
prayerPreview: Fear not, Zacharias, for your prayer is heard.
Lord, You did not despise the prayer of Your servant,
who prayed not for offspring he had ceased to hope for,

title: The Word Made Flesh in Her
subtitle: Gabriel comes to Nazareth, and the eternal Word descends into the Virgin's womb
prayerPreview: O Jesus, Son of the living God,
who by the will of the Father, the Holy Spirit working with Him,
came forth from that same Father's bosom

title: The Leaping of the Forerunner
subtitle: Mary comes to the hill country, and the child in Elizabeth's womb leaps for joy
prayerPreview: Holy John the Baptist,
you who were filled with God before you were born from the womb,
knowing God before you were known in the world:

title: The Book of His Generations
subtitle: The genealogy of the Saviour — how God descended to us through the line of Abraham and David
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, our hope and our trust,
be mindful of what You took upon Yourself for our redemption.
Be mindful that, the Creator of all things,

title: The Silence of Joseph
subtitle: Joseph, finding Mary with child, resolves to send her away in secret — until the angel speaks
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who art the impregnable wall of all who hope in thee,
be thou my refuge in tribulation;
behold my tribulations and my distresses, and have mercy on me,

title: The King Enrolled Among Us
subtitle: A decree goes out, and the Lord of eternity is counted in the census of Caesar
prayerPreview: Would that You, Lord Jesus,
would make me, a wretched one, to be among Your chosen,
that You might enroll me in eternity.

title: The Knife of Stone
subtitle: On the eighth day, the Infant sheds His first blood and is named Jesus, Savior
prayerPreview: O merciful Jesus, who, born of the Virgin under the Law, willed to be circumcised:
circumcise, O merciful Jesus, the thoughts, the words, and the works of Your servant,
that I may carry through nothing contrary to Your will in thinking, in speaking, in working.

title: The Star and the Three Kings
subtitle: The Epiphany — the Magi follow a new star from the East to the Child
prayerPreview: O good Jesus, born of the Virgin,
who revealed Yourself to the Magi with a star to lead them,
and made them return to their own country by another way:

title: The Light to the Nations
subtitle: Mary carries the Child into the Temple, and old Simeon takes him into his arms
prayerPreview: O Jesus, to be desired,
who in the temple gave yourself mercifully to just Simeon,
who longed to see you, that he might hold you:

title: The Flight into Egypt
subtitle: By night the Child is carried into exile, and the innocents are slain for His sake
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who, while yet an infant, willed to suffer persecutions and exile,
and willed that infants then be slain for Your sake:
grant to me, wretched one, to bear all these things patiently for You,

title: The Return from Egypt
subtitle: The weary road home through the wilderness, where the boy John already does penance
prayerPreview: O most gentle Jesus,
who, born of the Virgin, withdrew into the regions of Egypt,
and, called out of Egypt, returned into the land of Israel:

title: The Boy Lost and Found
subtitle: Jesus, twelve years old, remains behind in the Temple among the doctors
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, Son of the living God,
who for three days were sought by your grieving parents,
and at last were found in the Temple:

title: The Hidden Years
subtitle: The Lord Jesus from His twelfth year to the threshold of His thirtieth — the long silence of God among men
prayerPreview: O Jesus, meek, the very pattern of true patience,
and the example of humility,
drive far from me all swelling of pride,

title: The Voice in the Wilderness
subtitle: John the Baptist preaches repentance by the Jordan, and his whole life cries out the word of penance
prayerPreview: Most blessed John the Baptist, forerunner of Christ and most holy virgin,
who preached penance to sinners by your word
and showed it no less by your example,

title: The True Light
subtitle: John, sent from God, bears witness to the Light that shines in the darkness
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, Son of the living God, who art the true Light that enlightens every man coming into this world,
I, a wretch obscured by dense darkness, adore thee,
beseeching thy mercy, that thou wouldst enlighten my soul, form my mind, order my thoughts,

title: I Am Not the Christ
subtitle: John the Baptist confesses the truth before the priests and Levites — and turns the question on you
prayerPreview: Lord, Lord, open to us, for we stand knocking at your door.
We dare not call ourselves yours if we do not do your works — for that, as your servant says, would be a lie.
You have said: You are my friends, if you do what I command you; do not say to us, I do not know you.

title: The Tribunal of the Heart
subtitle: Ludolph on true penance: to weep for what is done, and do no more the things to be wept for
gospelRef: Luke 11:32 (the men of Nineveh); cf. Proverbs 18:3, Psalm 130:1
prayerPreview: Lord, the patience of your mercy has led me to repentance:
let me not waste it by delay.
You promised pardon to the one who repents,

title: The Lord Goes Down to Jordan
subtitle: The sinless One comes to be washed among sinners
prayerPreview: Will you be baptized, Lord Jesus?
For what, or what need have you of Baptism?
Does the healthy need medicine, or the clean need cleansing?

title: The Desert of Forty Days
subtitle: Christ led by the Spirit into the wilderness, fasting and tempted, that He might teach us to conquer
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, You went into the desert not for Your own need, but for ours.
Lead me by Your Holy Spirit, and not by any feigning of the evil one,
into the desert of my own heart and of quiet contemplation,

title: Behold the Lamb of God
subtitle: John points to Jesus at the Jordan: "Behold the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world"
prayerPreview: O Lamb of God,
acknowledge me, a wretched man,
among the sheep to be set at your right hand.

title: Come and See
subtitle: The first disciples leave John, follow the Lamb, and ask where He dwells
prayerPreview: O good Jesus, Redeemer of the lost, Saviour of the redeemed, hope of the exiled.
Sweet consolation of the poor in spirit, strength of the labouring, refreshment of the weary, crown of those who triumph.
The one reward of the citizens above, and their true gladness.

title: The Water Made Wine
subtitle: The wedding at Cana, where Christ honors marriage and turns water into wine
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who on the third day — that is, in the time of grace —
came in the flesh to the wedding,

title: The Cleansing of the Temple
subtitle: Jesus drives the sellers from His Father's house, and zeal for it consumes Him
prayerPreview: Brethren, let every Christian, in the members of Christ, be eaten up with zeal for the house of God.
Who is eaten up with zeal for the house of God?
He who, whatever he sees there gone wrong, strives to have it corrected, longs to see it amended, and is not at rest.

title: He Must Increase, I Must Decrease
subtitle: John the Baptist yields his glory to Christ at the Jordan, as his disciples murmur
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Master,
teach me the truth of life, of justice, and of doctrine —
of heart, of mouth, and of work —

title: The Light Goes Up Into Galilee
subtitle: Jesus withdraws into Galilee and begins to preach: "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand."
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who, having compassion on our misery, and as a true physician willing to give us the medicine of souls,
began your preaching from penance, and bade us, while we have time, to do it:

title: The Boat of the Obedient
subtitle: Christ teaches from Simon's boat, and the nets break with the catch
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
make me rush upon the nets of preaching and of desire and of good work
through eagerness to hear the word of God;

title: The Zeal of Christ in Preaching
subtitle: How Christ called fishermen, not the wise, to found His Church
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who as a diligent physician went about seeking the sick,
and healed the infirmities of bodies and of souls,

title: The Calling of Matthew
subtitle: Christ summons a tax-collector from his money-table, and feasts in his house
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who in many ways callest and admonishest me to follow thee,
kindle my heart by thy divine inspiration,
that by thy grace I may follow thee who callest,

title: The Choosing of the Twelve
subtitle: Christ keeps watch all night upon the mountain, and at dawn names His Apostles
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who out of your inestimable mercy came into the world,
to call sinners back from their wanderings to repentance,

title: Upon the Mountain
subtitle: Christ ascends the mountain and opens His mouth to teach the eight Beatitudes
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who, that You might teach the higher summits of the virtues,
ascended the mountain with Your disciples,

title: Salt of the Earth, Light of the World
subtitle: On the mountain, Christ tells His own they must shine in deed and word — and that He came not to break the Law but to fulfill it
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who to the men of old promised the things of time,
but to us things eternal, that our justice may overflow:
grant me to shine before You and before my neighbors in word and in deed,

title: The Other Cheek
subtitle: On the mountain, the Lord overturns the law of vengeance and commands a more terrible love
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, most gentle Master of all humility and patience,
grant to me, the least and simplest of Your servants,
that, setting myself low and beneath all others,

title: The Whip of Cords
subtitle: Christ drives the merchants from the temple, consumed with zeal for his Father's house
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, let the zeal of your house consume me.
Make a whip even of my own sins,
and drive out from the temple of my heart all that I keep there for sale —

title: Our Father
subtitle: The Lord teaches His disciples to pray on the mountain
prayerPreview: O Power, O Word of wondrous working!
Come now, my good Lord Jesus, Word of the Father,
complete Thou my prayer,

title: Treasure in Heaven
subtitle: On the mountain, Christ teaches: lay up no treasure on earth, but in heaven
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
make me lay up no earthly gain upon the earth,
but the rewards of merit in heaven;

title: Be Merciful, Judge Not
subtitle: On the mountainside: the Lord teaches mercy, the forbidding of judgment, and confidence in prayer
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who have taught us to show mercy to all, and to judge no one,
give me to fulfil what You teach,

title: The Narrow Way
subtitle: The Lord seals His sermon on the mountain: strive to enter by the strait gate
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, most merciful,
make me, by the narrow way of justice and the strait gate of penance, to enter into the hall of salvation and the life of glory.
Teach me to shun the deceit of the deceivers,

title: The Healing of the Leper
subtitle: Christ comes down from the mountain and stretches out his hand to touch what the Law cast out
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who came down from the mountain of the Father's throne
and from the virgin's womb

title: Speak But the Word
subtitle: The centurion of Capernaum pleads for his paralysed servant, and Christ marvels at his faith
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, I am not worthy that you should enter under the roof of my flesh,
for the boy of my sensuality lies paralysed, undone by the sickness of sin in the house of my body,
and is grievously tormented by the stirrings of its desires.

title: The Unclean Spirit Silenced
subtitle: In the synagogue at Capernaum, the Lord casts out the demon and is confessed even by hell
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, remove and cast out the unclean spirit from me,
that it may in no way be able to stain me, or to hold me fast in stains;
that I may be, and may appear, dead to the world,

title: The Widow of Nain
subtitle: At the city gate, the Lord meets a mother carrying her only son to the grave
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, come into Nain, into my soul tossed by temptations;
draw near to the gate, holding back the senses, that they may not pass into the affections;
come by grace, touch the heart by correction;

title: No Place to Lay His Head
subtitle: The scribe who came to Christ for gain, and the cost of following
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Master, searcher of hearts, and knower of secrets,
shut out and renew from me all craftiness and pretence,
and make me thy true and faithful disciple,

title: The Master Asleep in the Boat
subtitle: Christ sleeps in the stern while the storm rages, then rises and commands the wind and the sea
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
command the blasts and motions of suggestions and temptations;
come, and walk upon the waves of my heart,

title: Legion
subtitle: The two demoniacs of Gerasa, who cannot bear the presence of Christ
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, most kind lover of men,
I beseech thy goodness with tears:
free me from all the filth of my sins,

title: Lowered Through the Roof
subtitle: The paralytic let down through the opened roof, and forgiven before he was healed
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who in Your Passion climbed into the little boat of the Cross,
in Your Resurrection crossed the water,

title: If I Only Touch the Hem
subtitle: A woman who has bled twelve years reaches, trembling, for the fringe of His garment
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, I adore the feet of Thy mercy and Thy truth.
Most loving One, I beseech Thee:
heal by the touch of Thy grace my soul,

title: Son of David, Have Mercy
subtitle: The two blind men cry out, are touched, and see
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, light of eternal brightness,
enlighten my inward eyes,
that I may never fall asleep in the death of the soul;

title: The Harvest is Great
subtitle: Christ, moved with pity for the shepherdless crowds, sends out the Twelve
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who, sending Your disciples out to preach,
gave them power to heal, and precepts for living and conversing:

title: Sheep Among Wolves
subtitle: Christ sends out his disciples and arms them for patience in suffering
prayerPreview: Most merciful Lord Jesus Christ,
who sent your disciples to do battle against the world and the devil,
as sheep among wolves,

title: Fear Not Those Who Kill the Body
subtitle: Christ arms his friends against the fear of death and bids them confess him before men
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me so to despise all earthly desires and sinful delights
that I may fear to suffer nothing on earth for your name;

title: Fire Cast Upon the Earth
subtitle: Christ longs to be baptized in his own blood — and to set you free from every love that holds you back from him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, splendor of the Father's glory,
send into me the fire and fervor of the Holy Spirit,
that the love of God and of neighbor may be kindled, increased, and made perfect in me.

title: The Cup of Cold Water
subtitle: Christ promises that even a cup of cold water, given in His name, shall not lose its reward
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Master,
grant me so to venerate and receive, for your name's sake,
the prophets, who preach you by their teaching,

title: Are You the One?
subtitle: John, bound in prison, sends his disciples to ask the Lord — and Christ answers in deeds
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
since you are he who is to come to save us,
and we look for no other:

title: The Children in the Marketplace
subtitle: Christ rebukes the generation that would neither mourn with John nor rejoice with him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who by word and example have called us
to grieve for our sins and to rejoice for spiritual goods:

title: Return and Rest
subtitle: The apostles come back to Jesus from preaching, and the seventy-two are sent and return
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me, through the holy preaching of thy disciples,
so to be taught by thy grace,

title: Sorrowful Unto Death
subtitle: The first Compline of the Passion — Christ enters the garden of Gethsemane and begins to be afraid and heavy with grief
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who for me, a wretch, willed to be sorrowful and heavy:
grant me to bear all my sorrows unceasingly up to You;
and deign to bear them together with me, in union with Your own sorrow.

title: The Tears of the Magdalene
subtitle: A sinful woman washes the feet of Christ in the house of Simon
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, what she shamefully used for the odor of her flesh,
this she now offered to Thee in praise.
With my eyes I have sought Thee upon the earth.

title: The Better Part
subtitle: Mary sits at the Lord's feet while Martha serves
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who for us camest in the flesh,
through the union of the divine nature and the human,
let the bowels of thy mercy be moved over me,

title: Have Mercy on Us, Son of David
subtitle: Two blind men cry out on the road, and a mute man is freed from the demon that bound his tongue
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, light of eternal brightness,
illumine my inner eyes,
that I may never fall asleep in the death of the soul;

title: The Word That Heals from Afar
subtitle: Christ heals the official's dying son at Cana, with a single word
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who from Judea of angelic confession and praise came into Galilee, the wheel of this world:
for I am sick, tempted by the beauty of temporal things,

title: The Sower in the Boat
subtitle: Christ teaches the crowds from a little ship, scattering the seed of the Word
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, make me to go out from my old life,
that the seed of your Word be not trampled on the wayside of distraction,
nor wither on the rock of hard obstinacy,

title: Today This Scripture Is Fulfilled
subtitle: The Lord rises to read in the synagogue at Nazareth, and reveals that the prophet spoke of Him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who so humbled yourself to every service
that you did not disdain to take up the office of reader,

title: A Lamp Extinguished
subtitle: The beheading of John the Baptist in Herod's prison, amid a banquet
prayerPreview: My Lord and my God,
into your hands I commend my spirit.
As John, friend of the Bridegroom, gave his life for the truth,

title: The Five Loaves
subtitle: Christ feeds five thousand in the desert place
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
make me lift the eyes of my mind from carnal delights,
from earthly riches and worldly ambitions, up to You,

title: The Lord Who Fled the Crown
subtitle: Christ slips into the mountain alone, escaping the hands that would crown him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, you who resist the proud and give grace to the humble,
grant to all who take up the state of dignity and rule for the good of obedience, humbly and without ambition, and so persevere in it,
grace in the present, and glory in time to come;

title: On the Waves of the Night
subtitle: Jesus comes walking on the sea and lifts Peter from drowning
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, most merciful,
deign to climb into the little boat of my heart,
and still the windy gusts of pride and the storms of rising vices,

title: Seek Jesus for His Own Sake
subtitle: By the shore at Capernaum, the crowd that ate the loaves finds Him again — and He sees why they came
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, sufficient salvation of my soul,
grant me to desire You alone,
desiring You for Your own sake;

title: Lord of the Sabbath
subtitle: The hungry disciples pluck grain in the fields, and the Pharisees murmur
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, you are greater than the temple, for you are its Lord;
you are Lord even of the sabbath.
You will the affection of mercy and of pity

title: The Withered Hand
subtitle: A man with a shrivelled hand stands in the synagogue, and Christ says, "Stretch out your hand."
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
I beg you, by the depths of your mercy,
that you would mercifully heal me,

title: The Blind and Mute Man
subtitle: A man bound by a demon, blind and mute, is brought to Jesus and made whole
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus,
the devil I led into the house of my soul:
drive him out, and dwell there Yourself.

title: The Sign of Jonah
subtitle: The Pharisees demand a sign from heaven, and the Lord gives them only the sign of His own death and burial.
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Master, work in me the sign of Thy grace unto good,
that me, a wretched man, whom that great whale and dragon has swallowed up by sins spiritual and carnal, as by days and nights, and these three, by consent, by deed, and by habit,
Thou wouldst mercifully deign to deliver from his jaws.

title: Only Touch the Hem
subtitle: The bleeding woman reaches through the crowd for the edge of His garment
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, I adore the feet of Thy mercy and Thy truth:
I beseech Thee, most loving One, heal by the touch of Thy grace my soul, bloodied and stained with manifold sins.
Raise it up from the death of an evil and hidden will and purpose,

title: Tear Down the Barns
subtitle: Christ refuses to divide an inheritance, and warns against all greed
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, you would not be a judge of inheritance or a divider of estates,
for you came to gather, not to scatter,
to make many to have one heart and one soul.

title: The Rich Fool and His Barns
subtitle: A man begs Christ to divide his inheritance, and a rich man dreams of greater barns
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, You who are not the God of division, but of gathering, of peace, and of unity,
You who came to make men of one heart and one soul, that they might hold all things in common:
turn me away from the earthly, for whose sake You stooped to descend.

title: The Pool of Bethesda
subtitle: Christ finds the man who lay sick thirty-eight years
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
heal me, languishing, worn down by a long and evil sickness,
and paralytic, weighed down by a lasting numbness toward the good.

title: The Barren Tree
subtitle: The Lord seeks fruit on the fig tree, and finds none — yet stays the axe one more year
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me to do the fruit of good works fervently,
and to persevere in them without ceasing,

title: The Man Swollen with Dropsy
subtitle: Christ heals the dropsical man on the Sabbath, and calls the proud to the lowest place
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, take hold of me and protect me with the hand of your mercy,
lest the dropsy of carnal pleasure, or avarice, or pride, prevail in me;
and grant me, through a true humility of heart and mouth and work,

title: The Great Supper
subtitle: A man prepares an eternal feast, and the invited make their excuses
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who, willing that all men be saved,
have prepared for all the refreshment of heavenly blessedness,

title: The Feast of Tabernacles
subtitle: Jesus withdraws while his kinsmen urge him toward the world's glory
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me with heart and desire to go up to the festal day of the eternal solemnity,
and always to prepare myself for it,

title: The Woman Taken in Adultery
subtitle: Jesus stoops, writes in the dust, and condemns no one
prayerPreview: O merciful Jesus,
who didst mercifully free the woman taken in adultery from her accusers,
and, leaving her uncondemned, didst send her away in peace:

title: Light of the World
subtitle: In the temple, the hidden God speaks the words for which they take up stones
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who invite us to hear the word of God,
teach us to bear insults and injuries,
not to seek our own glory,

title: The Man Born Blind
subtitle: At the temple gate, the Light of the world anoints blind eyes with clay
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who gave light to the eyes of the man born blind,
give light, I beseech you, to the eyes of my heart,
that I may not stumble in the darkness, nor ever fall asleep in death.

title: The Door of the Sheepfold
subtitle: Christ teaches that no one enters the fold of the sheep except through the lowly door — through Him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Shepherd,
who for Thy sheep didst lay down Thy life as their ransom,
Thy flesh for their food, and Thy blood for their drink;

title: In the Porch of Solomon
subtitle: At the Feast of Dedication, the Jews surround Jesus in the temple, demanding plainly: are you the Christ?
prayerPreview: Take care, then, that you also,
while winter draws near,
while this present life is shaken by the storms of iniquity,

title: The Washing of Hands
subtitle: The Pharisees come down from Jerusalem to accuse the disciples, and the Lord lays bare their traditions against the commandment of God
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant me so to keep the commandments of God without transgression
that I may set them before all things, one and all,
and depart from them on no occasion whatever.

title: The Crumbs Beneath the Table
subtitle: A Canaanite woman cries after Christ for her tormented daughter, and will not be sent away
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, I ask thee and beseech thee, have mercy on me,
and help me in my necessities, and in the vexations of temptations and tribulations.
And if, Lord, because I am a dog, and before thee scarcely even as a dog,

title: Ephphetha — Be Opened
subtitle: Christ heals the deaf and mute man at the Sea of Galilee
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who at the last forsake the wicked,
come through the Sidon of preaching, to the sea of contrition,
of the Galilee of confession, of satisfaction, and of passing over,

title: Bread in the Wilderness
subtitle: Christ has compassion on the four thousand who would not leave Him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy upon the multitude:
upon the penitent, and the just who are beginning, and those advancing, the perfect and the contemplative,
who through a three days' contrition, confession, and satisfaction,

title: The One Bread in the Boat
subtitle: The Pharisees demand a sign from heaven; the disciples cross the lake having forgotten the bread
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, work in me the sign of Your weakness and Your Passion unto good,
that thereby, through You, I may rejoice to be helped and consoled.
O Bread of life, grant me always to be refreshed by Your love,

title: Take Up Your Cross
subtitle: Christ calls the crowd, and you among them, to follow Him by the way of the cross
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant me so to deny myself,
that in prosperity and in all things that delight for a time, I may turn away from evil, and seek nothing but Your honor;
and, renouncing my own will, that I may obey You, and for Your sake every creature.

title: The Light on Tabor
subtitle: Christ transfigured before three disciples on the high mountain
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, who upon the mountain were transfigured before Your disciples,
and made Your face to shine as the sun,
grant that, forsaking what is low and earthly,

title: Servants of No Account
subtitle: Christ descends the mountain to heal the boy the disciples could not free
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who taught us humility by word and example,
grant me that I may never think high things of myself, but always lowly things,
feel them in my heart, speak them with my mouth, and show them in my deeds.

title: The Coin in the Fish's Mouth
subtitle: At Capernaum the temple tax is demanded of the Lord, and a stater is drawn from the deep
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me to go to the sea,
calling my sins to mind in the bitterness of my soul;

title: The Millstone and the Little Ones
subtitle: Christ warns against scandalising the small in faith
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Master and ruler,
govern and rule mercifully all that is within me and without,
that never may scandal come from others to me, or from me to others;

title: The Shepherd Who Carries Me Home
subtitle: Christ goes out for the one lost sheep and bears it back on the wood of the Cross
prayerPreview: Come, Lord Jesus, seek your servant;
come, good Shepherd, seek your straying and weary sheep;
come, Spouse of Mother Church, seek the lost coin;

title: Seventy Times Seven
subtitle: Christ teaches how to win back the brother who has sinned, and how often to forgive
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me, unworthy as I am, in Your mercy,
that I may mend my sinning brother, correcting him with words in due season;

title: The King Who Settled Accounts
subtitle: The servant forgiven ten thousand talents, who would not forgive a hundred pence
prayerPreview: We run after Thee, O Lord Jesus,
hearing that Thou dost not spurn the poor, nor abhor the sinner.
Thou didst not abhor the thief confessing Thee,

title: The Indissoluble Bond
subtitle: By the Jordan, the Pharisees test Him on marriage, and He calls the soul to a higher continence
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who hast exhorted us to continence,
grant what Thou dost exhort,
and bestow on me, the frail one, chastity of mind and body;

title: What Do I Still Lack?
subtitle: The rich young man kneels before Jesus and asks the way to eternal life
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant to me — wretched and frail,
who until now have kept Thy commandments negligently and badly —
grant me at least from now on to keep them,

title: The Guarded Gateway
subtitle: The twelve evangelical counsels Christ added to the commandments
prayerPreview: Let the fear of God be to you more forceful than any necessity.
For if you are always willing to set the occasions before yourself,
you will keep nothing of the things commanded,

title: The Eye of the Needle
subtitle: Christ on the hard road of the rich, and the reward of those who leave all
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant to me, wretched and unworthy,
that for Your sake and for love of You,
and for the glorifying of Your name,

title: The Labourers in the Vineyard
subtitle: The householder goes out at every hour, and the wage is a single coin: eternal life
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, most high householder,
you led me into your vineyard early in the morning,
when from my youth you mercifully called me to faith and to your service,

title: The Steward Called to Account
subtitle: The wretched soul summoned to render the reckoning of its stewardship
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
make my soul a farm of virtues,
lest your steward, my reason, squander the goods you have entrusted to me;

title: The Rich Man and Lazarus at the Gate
subtitle: A man feasting in purple, a beggar full of sores lying at his door
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
since I am utterly poor in virtues, full of the sores of my sins,
a beggar laid before the gate of your mercy,

title: Lazarus, Our Friend, Sleeps
subtitle: The sisters send their grief to Christ, and He waits across the Jordan
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, I behold Thy wondrous goodness toward Thine own.
I do not say, Come and heal; I dare not command Thee.
I say only: behold, he whom Thou lovest is sick.

title: The Plot Against the Lord
subtitle: The priests and Pharisees gather in council to put Jesus to death
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, Angel of great counsel,
who permitted the council of the malicious to be formed against you,
and willed not to resist them by power, but rather to yield by patience:

title: The Ten Lepers
subtitle: Ten unclean men cry from afar, and one returns to give thanks
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, trusting in your tenderness,
I, like a leper, sprinkled over with the stains of many sins, flee to you, the merciful physician;
I, unclean, befouled with the spots of my vices, run to the fountain of mercies,

title: The Closed Door
subtitle: A Samaritan town refuses to receive the Lord on His way to Jerusalem
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who didst not avenge Thyself on the Samaritans who denied Thee welcome,
but, bearing it patiently, didst humbly turn aside to another place:

title: The Cup He Came to Drink
subtitle: On the road, going up to Jerusalem, He tells the Twelve in secret what awaits Him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who for our salvation willed to drink the cup of the Passion,
and to be drenched in the baptism of Your blood,

title: The Blind Man at Jericho
subtitle: A beggar by the road cries out, and the Light passes by
prayerPreview: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.
Lord, that I may see.
You who do not disdain the poor and the lowly, stand still for me.

title: The Man in the Sycamore
subtitle: Zacchaeus climbs the tree that is the Cross, and the Lord looks up at him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who looked with mercy on Zacchaeus climbing into the sycamore,
and calling him to yourself and turning aside into his house, healed him of every vice:

title: The Two Blind Men Beyond Jericho
subtitle: Two beggars at the roadside cry out, and the Light of light touches their eyes
prayerPreview: Light of light, and fountain of brightness, Lord Jesus,
who, moved with mercy at the love of the two blind men crying out to Thee,
didst deign to touch their eyes and so enlighten them:

title: The House of Obedience
subtitle: Mary breaks the alabaster and pours costly nard over the head of Christ at Bethany
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who, about to suffer for the whole world, willed to come and abide in Bethany, that is, in the house of obedience,
and there to sup, and there to let your head and your feet be anointed:

title: The Lord Seated upon the Colt
subtitle: Christ, the true Lamb, sets out for Jerusalem upon a borrowed beast
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who came of Your own will to the Passion,
come also to me, a wretched one, by grace into my heart;
and who then restored me by the cross, restore me, fallen again, by that same blessed Passion.

title: The King Comes Riding
subtitle: Palm Sunday — the multitude descends the Mount of Olives to meet a King upon a donkey's colt
prayerPreview: Do not fear; acknowledge him who is praised by you,
and do not tremble when he suffers,
because that blood is poured out

title: The Lord's Weeping over Jerusalem
subtitle: Christ draws near the city, and through his tears foresees its ruin
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who by word and by example
taught us to weep rather than to laugh,

title: The Cleansing of the Temple
subtitle: The Lord enters His Father's house and drives out the buyers and sellers a second time
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, Lord of the temple,
who came straight to Your Father's house and drove out all that defiled it,
made for Yourself a scourge of little cords and overturned the tables of greed,

title: The Widow's Two Coins
subtitle: Christ watching the treasury, as a poor widow gives her whole living
prayerPreview: O God, be merciful to me, a sinner.
I am not as the proud, who trusting in their own justice
pray thus within themselves, as though not before You:

title: The Withered Fig Tree
subtitle: Christ comes hungering for fruit, and finds only leaves
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant me to have not only leaves — that is, words and the esteem of justice —
but the fruit of works, that I may never incur Your curse.
Grant that, as Your true servant, I may follow You according to my small measure,

title: The Vineyard Let Out to Tenants
subtitle: The householder plants a vineyard, lets it to the husbandmen, and at last sends his Son
prayerPreview: The vineyard has been let out to us, O Lord,
and it is let out on this condition:
that we render the fruit to the Lord in his seasons,

title: The Vineyard and the Blood-Stained Tenants
subtitle: The householder plants his vineyard, sends his servants, and at last his Son — who is cast outside the wall and killed
prayerPreview: A vineyard has been let out to us, O Lord,
and let out on this condition:
that we render the fruit to you in its seasons.

title: The Wedding Garment
subtitle: The King enters to see the guests, and finds one without the wedding garment
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, King of heavenly glory,

who through Thy messengers, that is, through the sacred Scripture, through infirmities, through the peril of living, through the turning of fortune, and through Thy free will, hast called us to Thy marriage:
give us the wedding garment of charity,

title: The Image on the Coin
subtitle: The flatterers encircle Christ with the question of the tribute to Caesar
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
teach me to understand and to beware the cunning of seducers;
and to keep always the truth of life, of doctrine, and of justice.

title: The Greatest Commandment
subtitle: The lawyer's testing question, and the Lord's answer: Love
prayerPreview: Lord, he loves thee less who loves anything together with thee, which he loves not for thy sake.
Thou alone, without any rival, dost will to possess my heart.
Grant me to love thee with my whole heart, that it be inclined to no thing more than to thee.

title: In the Seat of Moses
subtitle: Christ warns the crowds: hear the teaching, do not imitate the life
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, in your mercy, take from me the failure of good works,
that I may deserve not only to think and to speak good things, but also to do them, and to fulfill them in deeds.
Remove from me austerity and cruelty,

title: The Eight Woes
subtitle: Christ pronounces eternal woe upon the hypocrites who shut heaven against men
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who give blessings to those who obey your Law and your precepts,
and maledictions to the disobedient:

title: The Signs Before the End
subtitle: On the Mount of Olives, facing the doomed Temple, the Lord foretells the signs of His coming
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, Judge of all who art to come,
grant me constancy in the truth of the faith,
that I be not seduced by feigned and false men,

title: The Abomination of Desolation
subtitle: The coming of Antichrist, and the flight to the mountains of virtue
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, in your mercy,
grant me, when I have seen the abominations of the world and the raging tribulations,
to flee to the mountains and the heights of the virtues;

title: Remedies Against the Last Deceptions
subtitle: Ludolph teaches the soul how to stand against false visions before the coming of the Lord
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, meek and humble of heart,
ground my heart in true humility,
and shut out from me every motion of presumption and pride,

title: Watching and Praying for the Coming
subtitle: The Lord teaches us to pray without ceasing, that His coming may not find us unprepared
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me to imitate the signs in the sun, moon, and stars,
that is, the examples of good works

title: The Sign of the Cross in Heaven
subtitle: Christ the Judge returns in majesty, the Cross blazing brighter than the sun
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who are to come to judge with power and great majesty,
while the Angels bear before You the sign of the glorious Cross as a triumphal banner,
seal my soul with the imprint of this most holy Cross;

title: Lift Up Your Heads
subtitle: The consolation of the elect as the Judge draws near, and the parable of the fig tree
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, come to me in the clouds of penitence,
and dissolve my heart into a rain of tears.
Cast out the demons that hold power over my vices,

title: The Day That Comes Without Warning
subtitle: As in the days of Noah and of Lot — the sudden coming of the Lord
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me to go before the uncertainty of the day of your coming
by watching and by working well,

title: The Watchful Householder
subtitle: The hour of the thief, and the soul keeping vigil against death
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
grant me unceasingly to ponder the day of my passing and the day of judgment,
and to foresee my last things,

title: Loins Girded, Lamps Burning
subtitle: Waiting through the night for the Master's return
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who commanded us both to bind fast our loins and to hold our lamps burning,
do You Yourself gird our loins,

title: The Ten Virgins
subtitle: At midnight the cry goes up: Behold, the Bridegroom comes
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Bridegroom,
grant me, with the wise virgins, to do good works with right intention,
and watchfully to persevere in them,

title: The Talents Entrusted
subtitle: The Lord, like a man going abroad, hands His servants His goods and departs — and returns to settle accounts
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who in mercy have handed over to us your gifts,
and warn us, watchful and diligent,

title: The Winnowing of the Threshing-Floor
subtitle: The Son of Man comes in majesty to separate the sheep from the goats
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, judge of the living and the dead,
in the last judgement deign to set me at thy right hand,
and then make me to hear that most sweet voice of thine,

title: Behold and Do
subtitle: On the threshold of the Passion: Christ, the pattern shown on the mountain
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who on the third day before the time of your Passion and death
foretold to your disciples your Pasch and your passing out of this world,

title: The Price of a Friend
subtitle: Judas goes out to the council and sells his Master for thirty pieces of silver
prayerPreview: O most merciful Jesus,
who bore the labors of preaching, the weariness of journeying,
the insults and reproaches, the injuries and the anguish, for our salvation,

title: The Supper Prepared
subtitle: At the first vespers of the Last Supper, two disciples go to make ready the upper room
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who at the hour of vespers kept the last supper with your disciples
in the great upper room, furnished,

title: The Washing of the Feet
subtitle: Christ rises from supper and stoops to wash the feet of his disciples
prayerPreview: O Jesus, gentle example of true humility,
who didst wash the feet of thy disciples,
I ask and beseech thee, Lord, purge my affections,

title: The Warning at the Table
subtitle: Christ, troubled in spirit, names the traitor among His own — and Judas goes out
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, good Shepherd,
who willed in many ways to call Your disciple back from his errors to repentance,
call me back from all my wanderings and from all my errors.

title: The Bread He Became
subtitle: In the upper room, after the Passover lamb, Christ gives Himself in bread and wine
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, who didst hand over and leave to us Thy very self,
Thy most holy Body and Blood, truly for our food and drink:
grant me, in receiving and before receiving this sacrament,

title: Let Not Your Heart Be Troubled
subtitle: After Judas goes out, the Lord consoles his own with his farewell at the Supper
prayerPreview: O Lord, prepare what you are preparing:
us, that is, for yourself, and yourself for us,
that there may be a multitude of mansions for the diversity of merits:

title: As If You Were Present
subtitle: On meditating the Passion of the Lord, in general
prayerPreview: Most sweet Lord Jesus,
pour into me, a sinner, I beseech You, the fullness of Your love;
that I may desire nothing earthly or carnal,

title: The Agony in the Garden
subtitle: Compline: Christ enters Gethsemane and is sorrowful unto death
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who for me, a wretch, willed to be sorrowful and heavy,
grant me to bear all my sorrows unceasingly toward you;

title: Matins: The First Interrogation
subtitle: The Lord, bound, struck on the cheek before Annas in the dark of night
prayerPreview: O Lord Jesus, how do You sit so despised and desolate?
Where are Your disciples and Your friends?
O my one good! O my one and only joy!

title: The Council of the Malignant
subtitle: At first light they bind Jesus and lead Him to His death
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who at the first hour of the day suffered yourself to be led, bound, into the council of the Jews,
and after many injuries and revilings to be led from Caiaphas to Pilate,

title: Terce: The Silent Lamb
subtitle: Christ stands mute before Pilate, then is scourged and crowned with thorns
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, who at the third hour of the day willed to be scourged for us,
free me from the scourges of that eternal wrath which I have deserved;
by the piercings of your most sacred head, pierce my mind,

title: The Place of the Skull
subtitle: The Sixth Hour: led outside the gate to Calvary, given wine mingled with gall
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, they gave you gall for your food,
and in your thirst they gave you vinegar to drink;
they sought to embitter the inmost heart of you, most sweet Lord.

title: Into Your Hands
subtitle: None: the Lord bows His head and gives up His spirit upon the Cross
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who at the ninth hour of the day, hanging upon the gibbet,
and crying with a loud voice, commended Your spirit into the hands of the Father,

title: The Lowering of the Body
subtitle: Vespers — Joseph of Arimathea takes the Lord down from the cross
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who at the evening hour willed to be taken down from the cross by Joseph,
and to be received between his arms;

title: The Anointing and the Tomb
subtitle: The hour of Compline: His body wrapped, carried, and laid in the new sepulchre
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who at the hour of Compline willed to be anointed and prepared with spices,
to be wrapped and bound in the shroud and the other linens,

title: The Praise of the Cross
subtitle: An epilogue to the Passion: how His suffering makes Him lovable to us
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who for the redemption of the world willed to bear patiently miseries and anguish,
reproaches and insults, slanders and injuries, pains and afflictions, the Passion and death:

title: The One Lamp Still Burning
subtitle: Holy Saturday — while all faith flickered, Mary alone kept the flame
prayerPreview: O good Jesus,
the ineffable mercy of Your tenderness was not yet satisfied,
until, breaking through the bars of hell also,

title: The New Day After the Sabbath
subtitle: Christ rises from the sealed tomb at the first light of morning
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, sole sweetness,
who, the chains of death being broken, glorified your body,
and rose in glory so unspeakable:

title: The First to See Him Risen
subtitle: Easter morning: the risen Lord comes first of all to His grieving Mother
gospelRef: Cf. John 21:25; Mark 16:9 (Ludolf's pious tradition of the Resurrection)
prayerPreview: O Mary, Mother of God and gracious Virgin,
true consoler of all the desolate who cry out to you:
by that great joy with which you were consoled,

title: At the Tomb Before Dawn
subtitle: The holy women come with their spices while it is still dark, seeking the Lord in the grave
gospelRef: Matthew 28; Mark 16; Luke 24; John 20 (esp. Matt 28:1; Mark 16:2; Luke 24:1; John 20:1)
prayerPreview: Let us therefore imitate these same holy and most devout women,
that as they with their spices sought the Lord
with their whole heart and desire in the sepulchre,

title: Mary, and the Voice That Knew Her
subtitle: The risen Lord speaks one word — her name — and turns her grief into joy
prayerPreview: But, O sweet Jesus, why do you so keep off from your sacred and most longed-for feet one who loves you?
Do not touch me, you say. Why, O Lord?
And why may I not touch those longed-for footprints of yours,

title: All Hail
subtitle: The risen Lord meets the three Marys on the road, and they hold his feet
prayerPreview: O Jesus, our redemption, our love and our longing,
who, rising from the dead, willed in your mercy to meet and appear
to the devout women seeking you, desiring you, returning from your tomb:

title: The Lie of the Guards
subtitle: The chief priests buy silence, and the soldiers swear to a theft they slept through
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
to blacken the fame of Your Resurrection the Jews gave a large sum of money to the soldiers, and corrupted them to pervert the truth.
Grant me that I be not corrupted by money, nor corrupt others,

title: He Sought, and Was Found
subtitle: The risen Lord appears to Peter, to Joseph in his prison, and to James who would not eat until he saw Him
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant that I may be Peter by obeying,
and James by trampling down my vices,
and Joseph, going from virtue to virtue by increase,

title: The Stranger on the Road
subtitle: Two disciples walk to Emmaus, grieving, and the risen Lord joins them unrecognized
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, faithful friend, faithful companion, kind Lord,
you could not bear to see your own so grieved and so astray.
When I am weighed down by perplexity and weariness,

title: Peace Be With You
subtitle: The risen Lord steps through closed doors and stands in the midst of his frightened disciples
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus, you who came in through the closed doors,
stand now in the midst of us.
You said to your own: Peace be with you —

title: The Wound and the Word
subtitle: The eighth day: Thomas touches the scars and cries out, "My Lord and my God"
prayerPreview: My Lord and my God.
Thou art my Lord, who redeemed me with Thy blood and made me Thine own;
Thou art my God, who created me and formed me from the slime of the earth.

title: The Lord on the Shore
subtitle: The risen Christ appears to seven disciples at the Sea of Tiberias
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ, grant me, a wretched man, to set before you the food you ask,
by obeying your commandments;
for you are in a manner refreshed by our obedience, and in it you take the greatest delight.

title: On the Mountain in Galilee
subtitle: The risen Lord appears to the eleven, and to more than five hundred brethren at once
prayerPreview: O Lord Jesus Christ, to whom is given all power in heaven and in earth,
exalted in your humanity, set at the right hand of the Father,
raised above all the angels,

title: The Forty Days
subtitle: Christ lingers with his own, proving the Resurrection true, before the Ascension
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
by your glorious Resurrection,
in which, living and immortal, you appeared to your disciples and faithful;

title: The Lord Ascends into Heaven
subtitle: The fortieth day: Christ is lifted up and sits at the right hand of the Father
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who hast deigned, through Scripture and the teaching of the Gospel,
to call us to the catholic faith,

title: The Struck Cheek
subtitle: Christ bound before Annas at the hour of Matins, and the blow upon his face
prayerPreview: Lord Jesus Christ,
who at the hour of Matins, before Annas,
received a blow from an officer,

title: The Fire That Does Not Consume
subtitle: Pentecost: the Holy Spirit descends in tongues of flame upon the disciples
prayerPreview: O Holy Spirit, the Spirit of truth,
come now and descend, fill us and console us,
strengthen us and instruct us,

title: Made to Praise
subtitle: The soul, created from nothing, learns why it exists: to praise its Maker without end
prayerPreview: O Father of mercies,
I know, and truly I confess,
that it would better befit a wretched sinner to lie prostrate before You,

title: The Falling-Asleep of the Virgin
subtitle: Mary's last years on Mount Sion, her dormition, and her bodily Assumption
prayerPreview: I beg you — if there is in you any heart of compassion —
consider with what love she was tormented,
with what longing this Virgin burned,

title: The Judge Returns
subtitle: The Final Judgement in the Valley of Josaphat — when Christ appears, gentle to the just and terrible to the unjust
prayerPreview: Most kindly Jesus,
who through the boundless patience of your goodness
wait for my amendment:

title: The Pains of Hell and the Glory of Heaven
subtitle: After the Last Judgment: the eternal loss of God, and the joy no tongue can tell
prayerPreview: But let us by no means suffer this, O only-begotten Son of God,
nor let us undergo that intolerable and dreadful punishment.
Let us not be driven from the honor of that blessed glory,

title: The Sealed Book
subtitle: Ludolph closes his Life of Christ and seals it in the Name of Jesus
prayerPreview: O Jesus Christ, Son of the living God,
for these things, and for all that we devoutly believe of you
and ought to believe,
